text.title: idempotent-write-tools Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for idempotent writes and preview/apply pairs.

rule.statement: Every tool whose `execute` mutates external state MUST accept a required `idempotency_key: str` parameter, persist `key -> result` for >= 24h, and short-circuit on key hit.
rule.rationale: Long agent loops retry on transient failures; without a key the retry cannot be distinguished from a fresh call, and the side effect runs twice. Stripe, AWS, and Square ship the same contract for the same reason.

rule.statement: Pure read tools (`get_*`, `search_*`, `list_*`) MUST NOT take an `idempotency_key` parameter; reads are safe to repeat.
rule.rationale: Bloating the schema with unused parameters confuses the model and costs tokens. Reads are already idempotent by nature.

rule.statement: The idempotency_key MUST be supplied by the agent (or upstream orchestrator) and MUST NOT be generated inside the tool execute function.
rule.rationale: Generating the key inside the tool defeats dedup — every retry gets a fresh key. The key must be stable across the lifetime of the logical operation.

rule.statement: Operations that delete data, charge money, deploy infrastructure, or send irreversible communications MUST be exposed as `*_preview` (no side effect) + `*_apply` (idempotent, requires preview_hash) pair; the single-call form is forbidden in the catalog.
rule.rationale: Splits the model's plan step from the commit step. Agent, guard agent, or human can inspect the preview before commit; the hash makes apply TOCTOU-safe.

rule.statement: `*_preview` MUST be a pure function (no side effects whatsoever); it may call upstream dry-run APIs but MUST NOT mutate caches, counters, or any observable state.
rule.rationale: If preview has any side effect, agents cannot run it speculatively, and the safety property collapses.
